links.targetTitle: Toolbar buttons must be styled via Tailwind arbitrary child selectors since they have no dedicated style props

title: The DocumentToolbar className prop merges with defaults via twMerge requiring ! prefix for overrides
content:
The `DocumentToolbar` accepts a `className` prop that is merged with the component's default Tailwind classes using `twMerge`. The defaults are:

```
flex h-12 w-full items-center justify-between rounded-xl border border-gray-200 bg-slate-50 px-4
```

To override these defaults, you must use the `!` (important) prefix on your custom classes:

```tsx
<DocumentToolbar className="!bg-[#181825] !border-white/10" />
```

Without the `!` prefix, `twMerge` may resolve conflicts in favor of the defaults depending on specificity rules. The `!` prefix forces your values to take precedence.

This is a standard pattern when working with component libraries that use `twMerge` — the library controls base styling and consumers override specific properties via important modifiers.
description: When customizing the toolbar container, classes pass through twMerge which resolves conflicts. The ! (important) prefix is needed to guarantee custom values win over the built-in defaults.
